program_name: Engineering Technology for Strategy and Security
admission_type: open access with mandatory admission test (free access, but requires passing a verification of personal readiness)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access (non-restricted) master's programme that requires meeting curricular credit requirements and passing a verification of the graduate's personal readiness unless exempt. Admission requires a bachelor's degree (or equivalent) of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, minimum credits in specified scientific sectors, and English at B2 level.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree (Laurea) or an equivalent qualification with at least 180 CFU/ECTS.
academic_requirements.curricular_credit_requirements: At least 36 CFU (ECTS) acquired at any university in scientific areas in mathematics, chemistry and physics, of which at least 30 CFU in basic training activities in Mathematics, Chemistry and Physics; AND at least 45 CFU acquired at any university program in engineering-specific scientific areas with specified SSD codes (e.g. BIO/07, CHIM/12, GEO series, ICAR series, ING-IND series, ING-INF series).
academic_requirements.minimum_grade_or_gpa: No minimum grade is required to apply, but a bachelor's degree final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (i.e. about 99/110) or an ECTS classification of 'A' or 'B' exempts the candidate from the admission test.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation before enrolment.

language_requirements.required_level: Adequate English proficiency of not less than CEFR B2 level.
accepted_proofs: An English certificate held by the student (B2 or higher, recognised by the University).
Passing the University's B2 English test (University Language Centre / CLAT).
A bachelor's degree conducted entirely in English (requirement waived in that case).
exemption_rules: The English requirement is waived if the bachelor's degree was conducted entirely in English.
Where no certificate is held, English proficiency is verified through the admission interview or the University's B2 test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Verified at the admission/pre-evaluation stage, via certificate, the University B2 test, an English-medium degree, or during the readiness interview.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of Adequacy of the Graduate's Personal Readiness (admission test). NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants who do not qualify for an automatic exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Assessment of the candidate's technical/scientific background from the bachelor's degree, specialised knowledge and professional experience, familiarity with modelling and AI/machine-learning applications, problem-solving capability, and readiness for interdisciplinary work.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: A.Y. 2026/27 sessions: 8 September 2026, 28 September 2026, 18 November 2026, 18 January 2027 and 26 February 2027.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] No explicit numerical passing score is published; the test is mandatory but described as not selective, with automatic exemption available by grade.
entry_exam_or_test.exemption: Candidates with a bachelor's degree final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum, or an ECTS 'A' (or 'A'/'B') classification, are automatically exempt.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Enrolment and fee payment follow the UniGe enrolment calendar after the curricular check and (where applicable) the admission test.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] Programme-specific first-year enrolment dates for A.Y. 2026/27 follow the central UniGe enrolment calendar; a programme-specific date was not separately published.
application_deadline_eu.notes: This is a free-access programme; EU/resident applicants are not ranked, but must satisfy the curricular requirements and take the admission test unless exempt.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
Engineering Technology for Strategy and Security is listed among the English-taught master's degrees in the UniGeApply non-EU pre-evaluation selection.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published. This programme is not among the three with a known fall-session early closure (Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Maritime Science and Technology, Computer Engineering bachelor).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window closed 20 March 2026.

required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply for non-EU pre-evaluation.
Evidence of the first-cycle qualification (180 CFU minimum).

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (list of exams with credits and grades)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded with study-career documentation (within 10 days of pre-enrolment) to verify the curricular requirements (36 + 45 CFU).

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or proof of English-medium instruction
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate, University B2 test, or a bachelor's degree taught entirely in English (which waives the requirement).
Required for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Completed after UniGeApply eligibility, before applying for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: This degree course is free access, but requires passing a test.

at least 36 CFU (according to ECTS) ... in the scientific areas [mathematics, chemistry, physics]; a minimum of 30 CFU must be in basic training activities in Mathematics, Chemistry and Physics; at least 45 CFU ... in engineering-specific areas (BIO/07, CHIM/12, GEO series, ICAR series, ING-IND series, ING-INF series).

Adequate English language proficiency of not less than level B2 is required and verified through interview, student-held certification, or the University's B2 test. The requirement is waived if the bachelor's degree was conducted entirely in English.
Test dates: 08 September 2026, 28 September 2026, 18 November 2026, 18 January 2027, 26 February 2027.

uncertainty_notes: Degree class is the LM/DS (Defense and Security) inter-class; confirm whether the official page also lists a paired sub-class.
No explicit numerical passing score is published; the readiness verification is mandatory but described as not selective, with automatic exemption by grade.
Programme-specific first-year EU enrolment dates were not separately published; the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window and the central enrolment calendar apply.
No programme-specific non-EU early closure is published; the programme follows the institution-wide fall close of 20 March 2026.
